name: Layla bint Abi Murrah
title: Mother of Ali Akbar
shortDescription: Wife of Imam Hussain (AS) and mother of Ali Akbar, the youth who most resembled the Prophet (PBUH).
fullStory:
Layla bint Abi Murrah ibn Urwa ibn Mas'ud al-Thaqafi was one of Imam Hussain's (AS) wives. Her grandfather Urwa was a prominent companion of the Prophet martyred in Ta'if.

Layla is the mother of Ali Akbar (AS); the youth who, in appearance, character, and speech, was the most similar person to the Messenger of God (PBUH).

Lady Layla's presence in Karbala is debated among historians. Some early sources do not mention her presence and believe she died before Karbala or stayed in Medina, but in many elegies and later sources, this mourning mother's presence in Karbala is noted.

It is narrated in elegies that when Ali Akbar went to the battlefield, Imam Hussain (AS) asked Layla to pray for her son in the tent. The tragedy of losing a youth like Ali Akbar has immortalized Layla's name as the symbol of mothers who lost young sons in Ashura history.
quoteArabic: يا لَيْلى! ادْعِي اللَّهَ لابْنِكِ... فَإِنِّي سَمِعْتُ جَدَّهُ يَقُولُ: دُعاءُ الأُمِّ مُسْتَجابٌ...
quote: (In elegies:) O Layla! Pray for your son, for I heard my grandfather say a mother's prayer is answered...
